sentences.text: Hey, Kelly,
how are you?
I'm doing well, how are you doing?
What?
Right.
Appreciate you jumping on.
Yeah, of course.
What part of the country in Boston?
Nice.
Very cool.
Yeah.
How about you?
I'm in Scottsdale, Arizona.
Oh, nice.
Yeah, my, uh, my, my, uh, sister and her boyfriend are actually out here and they live, uh, in New Hampshire but they flew out of Boston.
So.
Oh, nice close there.
But, yeah, so, well, cool.
I appreciate you jumping on.
I was kind of getting filled in from a, and it looks like you guys might be familiar with apartment IQ.
I think we might have one of your properties kind of lined up with Willow Bridge already.
Uh, Chestnut Tower.
Does that make sense or sound right?
Yeah, I'm not the asset manager on that, but I, um, had a, the multifamily portfolio with Joel Peterson who's on the west coast and I did the east coast.
Yeah.

So we try and stay on top of in general as many, um, you know, everything that's out there basically and make sure that we are able to kind of understand and ask questions where and, um, have those conversations with our, uh, property management teams.
Nice makes sense.
And I guess, um, as far as the call, you're just kind of looking to learn a little bit more about, it's just like kind of going on.
Do you, um, do you, do you know much about how our platform works?
So, I guess what kind of makes us great.
I'd love to hear it from you.
Ok.
Well, cool.
Um, it's a pretty easy conversation.
I always say that there are four things that make this product really unique.
Um The first thing being is that we're fully automated.
So there's zero manual input going into this whatsoever.
A totally automated experience for, for your, your, your team and any of your partners, which is great.
Um What's nice too is we actually update our data every single day.
So as you're aware, you know, properties out there, change rates regularly.
So our goal is that any time you want to understand the market, you can jump into our platform and see what's going on today.
Um What else is nice is we can actually track any property out there as long as they have a website to use the property management software, they don't have to be our customers whatsoever.
So we can truly track the entire market.

And for transparency, the way we get our data is our tool goes out the property websites, we collect the data every single night and more specifically, we actually go to the online leasing section of the property website, um, which is powered by that property management software.
So that's why I was saying in order for us to track a comp they do have to have a website and use a property management software.

And then, um, the fourth thing that I like to mention, just given the light of the, um, I guess the real page lawsuit and sort of the uh the antitrust lawsuit going on, but everything inside of apartment IQ is 100% publicly available data.
Um Nothing to do with the, the sharing of private semi private information, anything like that.
So, if that is a concern whatsoever, just know that um we have nothing to do with that.
Right.
So you're, it's for, uh in different words, you're scraping all the public, what's available to lease data every night on each property.
Exactly.
Yeah.

So our, our data updates every morning at 7 a.m. But yeah, I mean, we're, we're literally just reflecting um what, what a renter would see when they visit your website or visit a competitor's website.
Um We obviously package it in a much prettier format and a more actionable format.
But yeah, exactly.
It's, it's what a renter sees.
Um, it's scraped.
Yeah, it's, it's funny.
Um, whenever we started doing this like four or five years ago, I wasn't allowed to use the word scraping or scraper.
People said it's not like a very attractive word in the industry, but that's what it is.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Ok.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Let me, let me walk you through it here.
OK.
I'm taking notes
and I'll send you some collateral over afterwards too.

Like a Power Bi?
I, Do you like Excel?
Do you like kind of like a an in app experience like this?
I guess what's kind of your preferred method for for analyzing markets?
We're trying to go in the Power Bi I
um trying to go that route, not quite there
like we have, we have it for some properties but we are trying to go that route for all.
Nice.
Yeah, so I mean the reason I was asking is that I do just want you to know that however you prefer to get it, we can, we can deliver it that way.
So if you get Power B I up and running, we have an API that um you know, we could feed the data directly into there too.
Um But yeah, I mean, looking at the screen, I mean, this would be your apartment IQ landing page.
So each one of your properties would actually get their own individual property card and then we base the data off of that property specific concept.
Uh What's nice is that I'll let you know you're, you're always in complete control of your concept.
So typically, whenever we build these things that you would provide a list of comps that you've been tracking and we'd start there.
However, we do also have a comp matching algorithm where essentially we look at every property in the vicinity of your property.
We let you know how well you comp out and then you can make the decision if you wanna follow them or not.
But yeah, nonetheless, you're in control of it.
And what else is cool is you can always add or remove a property um, to your comp set directly inside the app itself as well.
Ok.
Um, so that, and, and so because it's, you're scraping uh, website, it's when a new lease up comes online, you can include that property as well once they're, once they have a website.
Yeah, exactly.
Yeah.
So we actually have a construction database.
Um, as soon as the property starts pre leasing, we add it to our organic database or, or not our organic database.
So our, our, our main database and for there, each account actually has a customer success manager attached to it.
And whenever that new property starts leasing up, we start tracking it, that customer success manager is notified on their accounts that we think that our system thinks might be a relevant follow.
So even if, even if your team's not necessarily up to date on that second, we would reach out and say, hey, you know, property agent went live, we think it would be a relevant follow for you.
Right.
Yeah.

We don't charge per seat.

So if you guys use, I think I saw my notes, you use greystar, use a few other PM CS.

So we would actually build these properties under a greystar landing page and then all your Greystar connects like the site teams, the regionals, they would access the data through that Greystar portal.
Whereas your, whereas your company, you would have your own landing page here and all of your properties would live centralized here.
So it's, it's a, it's a much kind of smoother user experience from that, from that perspective.
But yeah, we offer unlimited users, we can set up whoever you want, sorry to jump in.

But so we, the way that we're set up, um we have to have everything go through our properties so be the gray stars, you know, signing the contract setting us up and, and so if that's the case, we, we from a EW, still have that sort of, um, landing page capability that you're talking about.
Yeah, for sure.
Ok.

Yeah, that's typically that seems to be, I mean, with, with all of our graystar properties, a lot of the conversations start at the, at the ownership level and then I'll get in touch with like the regional the on site team kind of walk me through everything.

So yeah, you get your own landing page and they would have theirs too.

Uh The first report which we're on here is the market survey.
And then if you select the data drop down, you'll see three additional reports as well.

Um But before I do, I want you to know that all of the data inside of apartment IQ is filterable by these different options.
So you could look at the data based on a specific comp or even a subset of comps.

Uh We simply list all of your competitors across the top.

But I think we did a great job of, of making it as easy as possible to compare your property's performance versus the competition.

Um you know, we'll show you proximity, you built total number of units, we'll show you advertised occupancy and I'll come back to this in a second.
But then we'll also show you things like exposure percentage.

How many are vacant you can see here that we will provide some uh some uh transactional data as well.

Um We'll also let you know one of our properties make new units available.
You can see that we're showing that over the next seven days or the next 30 days.
And then lastly, the big one being is that we will let you know if there's the rent special or concession tied to the property or tied to a specific floor plan or a unit in the property as well.

And we do take this a step further and we will calculate net effective rent and net effective price per foot at the property level at the floor plan level and all the way down to the unit level as well.
Right.
So any initial questions here before I talk about advertised occupancy?
Um Can you look at what the, what this group of rants have done over time?
I can Yeah.

If you wanna look at specific properties, kind of trends over time, you can talk with them on what's really interesting though is May 21st.

So definitely help with the uh the long term trends in season alley.
That's great.
Yeah, so yeah, that'll be out May 21st, which I feel like I've been talking about this forever and it's hard to believe that it's a week away.

Um But yeah, just in terms of advertised occupancy, I I like to call this out because the reason we call this advertised occupancy instead of just occupancy is because it's based off of the known number of vacant units in the property.
So just because it is public data, not private data, we take the vacant units that are on the property website, we divide it by the total number of units in the property.
That's how we get the uh the advertised occupancy percentage.
So some properties out there, they hide availability.
Um very typical in a lease up, but it does happen with the stabilized properties as well.
Um If there is hidden availability, we, we can't account for it.
We don't know it's there.

Um So that's why I like the to call that out and just be transparent again, this is just being reflective of what's on a property website, right?
Ok, cool.

So, you know, 123 is available today tomorrow.
It's not, we assume that it has been leased.
That's the science there.

Um One really cool use case that, that, that a lot of our IM customers seem to like is in terms of uh pricing renewals.

You know, may 17th numbers.
What's cool here is you can actually take a look and see how the market's pricing their two bedrooms that are available during the same time frame.
So you get a little bit more of a, a better idea of what a renter can expect.

All of the data will populate based on what's available in July.

but yeah, that's the idea behind it.
